title: Remove ban on empty(...)
author: curry684
body:
As argued in https://github.com/ekino/phpstan-banned-code/pull/79#issuecomment-4023461575 it is a mystery to me why this plugin suddenly blocks `empty` by default in a minor version update, which is breaking behavior for many codebases. Creating an issue as the PR comment might go unspotted.

PHPStan allows `empty` on max level itself, because disallowing it is part of [PHPStan Strict Rules](https://github.com/phpstan/phpstan-strict-rules), which is a set of rules *"for those who want additional safety in extremely defensive programming"*. It is paired there as `disallowedEmpty` with a ton of other similar constructs that have the same potential flaws (loose comparisons and short ternary). Including that plugin is an action somebody performs when they are aware they want to make their code not just safe from debug leftovers (like this plugin) but also consciously apply, to quote its title, *"Extra strict and opinionated rules for PHPStan"*. Opinionated is key there: it's not a universally accepted truth that `empty` is evil.

As `empty` is on its own not universally accepted a something that should be banned, like echo, die, eval and var_dump, it has no place in this plugin, and certainly not enabled by default.

title: How to ignore errors for enable non-ignorable rule?
author: kayw-geek
body:
In some scenarios, the functions declared in the rule have to be used, but the current rule is set to nonIgnorable, which makes it impossible to ignore the error.

Root Cause by the line:
https://github.com/ekino/phpstan-banned-code/blob/e7bb53abf376e97ea590eae94909480f6b16232b/src/Rules/BannedNodesErrorBuilder.php#L38

title: Prevent baseline exclusion
author: Spomky
body:
Hello,

`staab/phpstan-todo-by` allows to prevent errors from being present in the baseline (and [an option](https://github.com/staabm/phpstan-todo-by?tab=readme-ov-file#non-ignorable-errors) to disable this behavior).
This does not seem to be the case for this extension.

**❓ Question**: Is it possible to add this functionality here too?

Many thanks.
Kind regards.

title: Advanced configuration doesn't work
author: SamMousa
body:
It seems advanced configuration is not easy to get going.
According to PHPstan docs you shouldn't use extension installer when not wanting all rules of an extension.
I tried both with and without.

What I think is happening is that the list of banned nodes from the extension config is merged with the list specified in my project config. (Similar to how `ignoreErrors` is merged from the baseline file and project config).

This means that removing an item because I don't care about `echo` usage is not possible.

A workaround is to ignore the specific error manually, but doesn't feel very clean.

title: Make class prefix for tests configurable?
author: temp
body: Our tests don't start with `Tests\`, but with `App\Tests\`. It would be nice if this would be configurable, for `use_from_tests`.

title: How would one disable Else and ElseIf ?
author: JBx0
body:
Tackling some code mishabits I'd love to disable else and elseif aka enable detection.
How would one do that ?

title: PHP 8 support
author: hktr92
body: With PHP 8 around the corner, it would be nice to have support for it. Even if it's still in RC, I'm checking the compatibility of my project with this version.

title: How can I disable detection of die/exit ?
author: ywcdiao
body:
Hi Ekino,

As the detection of die/exit is enabled by default in the extension.neon.
Is it possible to disable such detection in my project? If so, how to do it?

Best regards,
Weichao

title: Support class bann rules
author: staabm
body:
Would be great if one could also ban single classes or even namespaces.

This is especially usefull when you wanne get rid of a external dependency in which you cannot annotate the classes itself with `@deprecated` as you dont own the code

If already supported: a example in the readme would be great

author: GrahamCampbell
body:
PHP supports shell_exec via backticks. Can this be detected?

---

title: Internal error: Expected instance of PhpParser\Node\Name for $node->name, PhpParser\Node\Expr\ArrayDimFetch given
author: jdeniau
body:
Hi, I got this weird error when trying to use your package.

The result of the debug is this:

```
Note: Using configuration file <path>/phpstan.neon.
<path>/src/Mapado/TicketingBundle/Export/ProTicketListGenerator.php
PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught RuntimeException: Expected instance of PhpParser\Node\Name for $node->name, PhpParser\Node\Expr\ArrayDimFetch given in <path>/vendor/ekino/phpstan-banned-code/src/Rules/BannedNodesRule.php:64
Stack trace:
#0 <path>/vendor/phpstan/phpstan/src/Analyser/Analyser.php(154): Ekino\PHPStanBannedCode\Rules\BannedNodesRule->processNode(Object(PhpParser\Node\Expr\FuncCall), Object(PHPStan\Analyser\Scope))
#1 <path>/vendor/phpstan/phpstan/src/Analyser/NodeScopeResolver.php(1691): PHPStan\Analyser\Analyser->PHPStan\Analyser\{closure}(Object(PhpParser\Node\Expr\FuncCall), Object(PHPStan\Analyser\Scope))
#2 <path>/vendor/phpstan/phpstan/src/Analyser/NodeScopeResolver.php(1139): PHPStan\Analyser\NodeScopeResolver->callNodeCallbackWithExpression(Object(Closure), Object(PhpParser\Node\Expr\FuncCall), Object(PHPStan\Analyser\Scope), Object(PHPStan\Analyser\ExpressionContext))
#3 <path>/vendor/phpstan/phpstan/s in <path>/vendor/ekino/phpstan-banned-code/src/Rules/BannedNodesRule.php on line 64
```

I do not know where it comes from, I try to parse a 1500 lines file :/

title: [Feature request] General setup to ban any statement
author: jasny
body:
It would be nice if you'd be able to ban any type of statement/expression, similar to banning functions

```php
banned_code:
  statements:
    - echo
    - goto
    - exit
    - eval
  functions:
    - ...
```

title: [Feature request] Detect echo
author: jasny
body: When using PSR-7, you mustn't use `echo` in your application.
